reasoning: The Lean statement captures the informal Putnam 1971 A1 claim accurately. (1) `S : Set (ℤ × ℤ × ℤ)` with `S.ncard = 9` correctly encodes "a set of 9 lattice points in 3D" — since `Set.ncard` has junk value 0 for infinite sets, the equation to 9 forces finiteness and exactly 9 elements. (2) The function L is defined using `t ∈ Ioo 0 1` (open interval), so L(P,Q) is the open line segment between P and Q, excluding endpoints — this matches "interior of the line segment". (3) The conclusion asserts existence of integers x, y, z and distinct P, Q ∈ S such that the real embedding ((x:ℝ),(y:ℝ),(z:ℝ)) lies in L(P,Q), which is precisely "a lattice point along the interior of the segment joining two distinct points of S". The coercions from ℤ to ℝ are handled correctly. No extra or missing hypotheses, and the quantifier structure matches.
